characters.name: Mira
characters.description: woman in her twenties, dark hair tied back, patched red wool cloak, heavy canvas pack, dented brass lantern hanging from the pack

scenes.title: Testing the broken bridge
scenes.source_excerpt: By midday the forest gave way to a river, wide and brown and loud. The old bridge had rotted; only two ropes and a few slick planks remained, swaying above the current. Mira tested the first plank with her toe. It groaned but held.
scenes.prompt: A handheld shot watches from the far bank across a wide brown river, framing the young woman low as she crouches and reaches a boot toward the first plank of a rotted rope bridge. The camera drifts unsteadily and tightens on her toe pressing the slick, groaning board, then tilts up to her white-knuckled hand clamped on the wet rope — the heavy pack dragging at her back, patched red cloak whipping toward the lens. Only two ropes and a few wet boards sway between her and the camera over the churning current. She mutters under her breath toward the crossing, "Hold for me. Just hold." Bright flat noon light, cold spray glinting between her and the lens, muted greens and browns, as [the river roars and churns, the old plank groans under her boot, wind buffets across open water].
scenes.negative_prompt: text overlay, subtitles, warped hands, extra people, night, calm water

scenes.title: The plank breaks
scenes.source_excerpt: Halfway across a plank cracked and dropped away, and she froze, one foot over nothing, breath caught hard in her chest. Then she found the next board with her boot and moved again, slow and certain, until at last she stumbled onto the far bank and lay in the mud, laughing and gasping at the bright noon sky.
scenes.prompt: The camera rides close and handheld on the bridge as a plank cracks and drops away beneath her, then whips from her white-knuckled grip up to her face as she freezes, one boot swinging over churning brown water. Holding tight on her clenched jaw, it stays with her as she breathes through her teeth, "Almost there. Almost," and feels out the next board with her dangling foot. As she lunges the last step, the camera pulls back and widens, following her stumble onto the far bank where she collapses into the mud, gasping up into the lens. Harsh noon daylight, wet sheen on everything, as [a sharp wooden crack and splash cut through the roaring current, her breath rasps, then breaks into breathless laughter as mud squelches].
scenes.negative_prompt: text overlay, subtitles, warped hands, extra people, calm expression, static background
